name: redwood
description: Redwood provides a first-class CLI that helps you at every stage of development, from your first commit to your first deploy

subcommands.name: dev
subcommands.description: Start development servers for api and web

name: --forward
--fwd
options.description: String of one or more Webpack Dev Server config options

args.description: Options to pass to Webpack Dev Server

subcommands.name: scaffold
subcommands.description: Generate Pages, SDL, and Services files based on a given DB schema Model. Also accepts <path/model>

-f
options.description: Overwrite existing files

--ts
options.description: Generate TypeScript files Enabled by default if we detect your project is TypeScript

args.name: model
args.description: Model to scaffold. You can also use <path/model> to nest files by type at the given path directory (or directories). For example, redwood g scaffold admin/post

subcommands.name: exec
subcommands.description: Execute scripts generated by yarn redwood generate script to run one-off operations, long-running jobs, or utility scripts

args.name: script
args.description: Name of the script to execute

script: until [[ -f redwood.toml ]] || [[ $PWD = '/' ]]; do cd ..; done; ls -1p scripts/

subcommands.name: build
subcommands.description: Build the api and web

name: --stats
options.description: Use Webpack Bundle Analyzer to visualize the size of Webpack output files via an interactive zoomable treemap

name: --verbose
-v
options.description: Print more information while building

subcommands.name: diagnostics
subcommands.description: Get structural diagnostics for a Redwood project (experimental)

subcommands.name: console
subcommands.description: Launch an interactive Redwood shell (experimental)

subcommands.name: info
subcommands.description: Print your system environment information

subcommands.name: upgrade
subcommands.description: Upgrade all @redwoodjs packages via an interactive CLI

name: --dry-run
-d
options.description: Check for outdated packages without upgrading

name: --tag
-t
options.description: Choices are "canary", "rc", or a specific version (e.g. "0.19.3"). WARNING: Unstable releases in the case of "canary" and "rc", which will force upgrade packages to the most recent release of the specified tag

suggestions.name: canary
suggestions.description: Upgrade to the latest canary release

suggestions.name: rc
suggestions.description: Upgrade to the latest RC release

name: --pr
options.description: Installs packages for the given PR

subcommands.name: serve
subcommands.description: Runs a server that serves both the api and the web sides

name: --port
options.description: What port should the server run on [default: 8911]

args.name: port
args.description: Port to run the server on

name: --socket
options.description: The socket the server should run. This takes precedence over port

args.name: socket
args.description: Socket to run the server on

name: --apiRootPath
options.description: The root path where your api functions are served

name: --apiHost
options.description: Forwards requests from the apiProxyPath (defined in redwood.toml) to the specified host

args.name: side
args.description: Which side(s) to check (Default to both)

subcommands.name: type-check
subcommands.description: Runs a TypeScript compiler check on both the api and the web sides

args.name: side
args.description: Which side(s) to check (Default to both)

subcommands.name: test
subcommands.description: Run Jest tests for api and web

name: --help
options.description: Show help

name: --version
options.description: Show version number

name: --watch
options.description: Run tests related to changed files based on hg/git (uncommitted files). Specify the name or path to a file to focus on a specific set of tests [default: true]

name: --watchAll
options.description: Run all tests

name: --collectCoverage
options.description: Show test coverage summary and output info to coverage directory in project root. See this directory for an .html coverage report

name: --clearCache
options.description: Delete the Jest cache directory and exit without running tests

name: --db-push
options.description: Syncs the test database with your Prisma schema without requiring a migration. It creates a test database if it doesn't already exist [default: true]. This flag is ignored if your project doesn't have an api side

args.name: sides or filter
args.description: Which side(s) to test, and/or a regular expression to match against your test files to filter by

subcommands.name: diff
subcommands.description: Compares the database schema from two arbitrary sources, and outputs the differences either as a human-readable summary (by default) or an executable script

name: -h
--help
options.description: Display this help message

name: --from-url
options.description: A datasource url

name: --to-url
options.description: A datasource url

name: --from-empty
options.description: Flag to assume from is an empty datamodel

name: --to-empty
options.description: Flag to assume to is an empty datamodel

name: --from-schema-datamodel
options.description: Path to a Prisma schema file, uses the 'datamodel' for the diff

name: --to-schema-datamodel
options.description: Path to a Prisma schema file, uses the 'datamodel' for the diff

name: --from-schema-datasource
options.description: Path to a Prisma schema file, uses the 'datasource url' for the diff

name: --to-schema-datasource
options.description: Path to a Prisma schema file, uses the 'datasource url' for the diff

name: --from-migrations
options.description: Path to the Prisma Migrate migrations directory

name: --to-migrations
options.description: Path to the Prisma Migrate migrations directory

name: --shadow-database-url
options.description: URL for the shadow database. Only required if using --from-migrations or --to-migrations

name: --script
options.description: Render a SQL script to stdout instead of the default human readable summary (not supported on MongoDB)

name: --exit-code
options.description: Change the exit code behavior to signal if the diff is empty or not (Empty: 0, Error: 1, Not empty: 2). Default behavior is Success: 0, Error: 1.`

subcommands.name: db
subcommands.description: Manage your database schema and lifecycle (Preview)

--help
options.description: Display this help message

name: --schema
options.description: Custom path to your Prisma schema

name: --force
options.description: Ignore current Prisma schema file

name: --print
options.description: Print the introspected Prisma schema to stdout

name: --url
options.description: The datasource url

name: --composite-type-depth
options.description: Specify the depth for introspecting composite types (e.g. Embedded Documents in MongoDB). Number, default is -1 for infinite depth, 0 = off

subcommands.name: push
subcommands.description: This command pushes the state of your Prisma schema file to the database without using migrations files

name: -h
--help
options.description: Display this help message

name: --schema
options.description: Custom path to your Prisma schema

name: --skip-generate
options.description: Skip generation of artifacts such as Prisma Client

name: --force-reset
options.description: Resets the database and then updates the schema - useful if you need to start from scratch due to unexecutable migrations

name: --accept-data-loss
options.description: Ignore data loss warnings. This option is required if as a result of making the schema changes, data may be lost
